changes_made: Renamed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_audit_logging_configured for clarity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls related to system monitoring and risk management. However, some naming improvements were necessary for clarity and specificity. Audit logging and network policy monitoring are applicable to Kubernetes, but the names needed refinement to better describe their purpose and ensure consistency with naming conventions.
naming_improvements: Use 'configured' instead of 'enabled' for audit logging to indicate setup
Specify 'traffic' in network policy monitoring to clarify the monitoring aspect
title: System Monitoring | Risk for Individuals
step1_reasoning: The control SI-4 (19) focuses on system monitoring and risk for individuals, which can be mapped to Kubernetes through audit logging and network monitoring capabilities. Kubernetes audit logging ensures that all API interactions are logged, which is akin to cloud services like AWS CloudTrail. Ensuring the integrity of these logs is crucial, hence the log integrity verification. Admission controllers can enforce security policies that help in monitoring and mitigating risks at the admission stage. NetworkPolicies can be used to monitor and control network traffic, similar to cloud threat detection services.
